task_completion.description: Navigate to an axial slice of the brain MRI where the lateral ventricles are visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main viewer shows an axial (top-down) slice of the brain. The lateral ventricles (dark, fluid-filled, roughly symmetrical structures in the central region of the brain) must be clearly visible.

task_completion.description: Create an arrow markup on the MRI slice.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graphical arrow markup is drawn and clearly visible overlaid on the MRI image in the viewer.

task_completion.description: Label the arrow markup as 'Left Lateral Ventricle'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The exact text 'Left Lateral Ventricle' is visible on the image, attached to or immediately adjacent to the arrow markup.

task_completion.description: Position the arrow to point to the left lateral ventricle.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tip of the arrow points directly to the left lateral ventricle. Note: In standard radiological imaging convention, the patient's left side appears on the right side of the image, so the arrow should point to the ventricle structure on the right side of the screen.

task_completion.description: Save a screenshot of the annotated view to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, the software's Screen Capture module, or a file explorer/terminal shows the image being saved or existing as 'ventricle_annotation.png' specifically in the '~/Documents/SlicerData/Screenshots/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the actual medical imaging software (e.g., 3D Slicer) to perform the annotation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D Slicer interface is visible with its standard toolbars, module panels, and slice viewers, rather than a generic image editing tool like GIMP or MS Paint.

integrity.description: Verify the annotation was created within the 3D viewer environment.
integrity.visual_evidence: The arrow and text are rendered as part of the software's native markup/annotation system over the MRI slice, rather than flat text/paint added post-hoc to a static image.

integrity.description: Verify the screenshot was captured and saved properly rather than faking a file creation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of the screen capture process (like the Screen Capture module open) or the final image file actually containing the annotated viewer.
